content.text: How can I run a specific sound analysis algorithm on an audio file and save the results in the MIREX format using command-line arguments?

This function initializes argument parsing with `argparse` to allow users to specify algorithms (`-bid`, `-lid`) and files, then executes the main processing logic defined by MSAF.

```python
def main():
    """Main function to parse the arguments and call the main process."""
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Runs the speficied algorithm(s) on the input file and "
        "the results using the MIREX format.",
        formatter_class=argparse.ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter)
    parser.add_argument("-bid",
                        action="store",
                        help="Boundary algorithm identifier",
                        dest="boundaries_id",
                        default=msaf.config.default_bound_id,
                        choices=["gt"] +
                        msaf.io.get_all_boundary_algorithms())
    parser.add_argument("-lid",
                        action="store",
                        help="Label algorithm identifier",
                        dest="labels_id",
                        default=msaf.config.default_label_id,
                        choices=msaf.io.get_all_label_algorithms())
    parser.add_argument("-i",
                        action="store",
                        dest="in_file",
                        help="Input audio file")
    parser.add_argument("-o",
                        action="store",
                        dest="out_file",
                        help="Output file with the results",
                        default="out.txt")

    args = parser.parse_args()
    start_time = time.time()

    # Setup the logger
    logging.basicConfig(format='%(asctime)s: %(levelname)s: %(message)s',
                        level=logging.INFO)

    # Run MSAF
    params = {
        "annot_beats": False,
        "feature": "cqt",
        "framesync": False,
        "boundaries_id": args.boundaries_id,
        "labels_id": args.labels_id,
        "n_jobs": 1,
        "hier": False,
        "sonify_bounds": False,
        "plot": False
    }
    res = msaf.run.process(args.in_file, **params)
    msaf.io.write_mirex(res[0], res[1], args.out_file)

    # Done!
    logging.info("Done! Took %.2f seconds." % (time.time() - start_time))
```

The code centralizes configuration within a `params` dictionary before calling `msaf.run.process`, ensuring all required settings are passed correctly and measuring performance using `time.time()`.
If I run this function without providing the input file using `-i`, how will it handle that missing argument?

Since '-i' has no default value defined in the code, attempting to call `msaf.run.process(args.in_file, ...)` without providing the input file via the command line will likely result in an error depending on how 'argparse' is configured or if it treats the missing argument as mandatory.
That makes sense, thank you for the clarification.
